title: Animals of the World
objective: Students will explore animals of the world concepts including The Red Queen Hypothesis.

steps.content: Let's explore The Red Queen Hypothesis! Have you ever wondered about this?

Introduce the Red Queen hypothesis — species must keep evolving just to maintain fitness relative to co-evolving partners; describe predator-prey arms races (cheetah speed vs gazelle speed, bat echolocation vs moth hearing jamming) and parasite-host co-evolution (myxomatosis in rabbits); explain Darwin's hawk moth and orchid as a classic example of mutualistic co-evolution predicting an unknown species; understand that co-evolution is a major driver of biological diversification
Can you explain the idea of an evolutionary arms race — how a predator and its prey keep evolving better attack and defence strategies in response to each other, never quite getting ahead?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about The Red Queen Hypothesis. You now know more about The Red Queen Hypothesis!
